title: Add a repository list panel to a dashboard

blocks.content:
In this milestone, you add a dashboard panel that displays GitHub repositories for an organization. This visualization helps you monitor repository details, including owner, URL, and forks.

Sign in to your Grafana Cloud environment, for example `mystack.grafana.net`.
Navigate to **Dashboards** from the main menu.

Open the panel editor by doing one of the following:

- If you see an **Add visualization** button or placeholder, click it.
- If you see a **+** icon in the top toolbar, click it and select **Visualization**.

Either option opens the panel editor.
Click the **data source** dropdown and select your **GitHub** data source from the list.
In the query builder, click the **Query Type** dropdown and choose **Repositories**.

blocks.reftarget: [data-testid='data-testid Panel editor option pane field input Title']
blocks.content: In the **Panel options** on the right, enter `GitHub Complete Repository Overview` in the **Title** field.
Click the **time picker** and select an appropriate range, for example **Last 30 days**.
Click **Save dashboard** to save the panel to your dashboard.

Your dashboard now includes comprehensive repository details. You can monitor forks, creation date, and owner details.

In the next milestone, you'll add a panel for GitHub issues data.
